last_completed_round.direction_name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 34 spent one bounded budget unit on a PTX-local export-scratch change and got a clear answer: correctness stayed intact, long-scoreboard dipped slightly from 7.23% to 7.08%, but barrier stall nudged up from 5.49% to 5.54% and end-to-end runtime regressed from 24.171008 ms to 24.339969 ms. That is not a promising live surface to continue exploiting. The correct next move is to restore the exact 489574e PTX anchor so the loop returns to the accepted 24.164272 ms base before it chooses a different aggressive family.
